prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ETS', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.6244) and high seasonality variance (0.8113), with ETS leading trend tracking and THETA dominating seasonal capture. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon models, indicating horizon-specific optimization is valuable. The combination of these factors strongly supports DBA combination method to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses.
prompts.skeptic:

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ETS",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_DWT_svr",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "late": "FT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_DWT_svr", "FT_rf", "ONLY_FT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "THETA", "DWT_svr", "ETS", "ONLY_FT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "FT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "FT_svr", "rf", "DWT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.6244,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.6244) and high seasonality variance (0.8113), with ETS leading trend tracking and THETA dominating seasonal capture. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon models, indicating horizon-specific optimization is valuable. The combination of these factors strongly supports DBA combination method to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (seasonality_corr_variance=0.8113). With only n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.35 on inverse_rmse_weights prevents overfitting. Relative spread mean=0.0717 shows meaningful model disagreement justifying diverse combination strategies. RMSE spread ratio=0.6244 supports rmse_focus preset."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set is diverse with 3 types: selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), baseline (dba_combination), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). No leakage issues - baselines never leak, and weighted methods use expanding rolling selection (train_window=5) from validation_summary.n_windows=3 windows. No redundancy pruning needed (<4 same-type candidates). Universe leaderboards show top performers but current set already includes best_per_horizon_by_validation which leads MAPE/SMAPE leaderboards.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.6244) and high seasonality variance (0.8113), with ETS leading trend tracking and THETA dominating seasonal capture. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon models, indicating horizon-specific optimization is valuable. The combination of these factors strongly supports DBA combination method to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (seasonality_corr_variance=0.8113). With only n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.35 on inverse_rmse_weights prevents overfitting. Relative spread mean=0.0717 shows meaningful model disagreement justifying diverse combination strategies. RMSE spread ratio=0.6244 supports rmse_focus preset.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set is diverse with 3 types: selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), baseline (dba_combination), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). No leakage issues - baselines never leak, and weighted methods use expanding rolling selection (train_window=5) from validation_summary.n_windows=3 windows. No redundancy pruning needed (<4 same-type candidates). Universe leaderboards show top performers but current set already includes best_per_horizon_by_validation which leads MAPE/SMAPE leaderboards.
